(&#8220;UOL&#8221;), referred to, together with its subsidiaries, as the &#8220;PagSeguro Group&#8221; or the &#8220;Group&#8221;, and was incorporated on July 19, 2017. (&#8220;PagSeguro Brazil&#8221;) were contributed to PagSeguro Digital on January 4, 2018 and in August, 2025 the PagSeguro Brazil shares was transferred to BS Holding Financeira Ltda (&#8220;BS Holding&#8221;), a subsidiary of PagSeguro Digital.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">In June 2024, PagSeguro Digital acquired 5% of Fundo de Investimento em Direitos Credit&#243;rios &#8211; PagSeguro (&#8220;FIDC&#8221;) shares from its subsidiary PagSeguro Brazil, which together with the 15% of FIDC shares previously acquired resulted in PagSeguro Digital owning 20% of the share capital of the fund.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; The objective of this fund is to anticipate third-party assignments in accordance with market operations.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:14.2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:14.2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">In January and February, 2025, the subsidiaries Yam&#237; and Zygo was incorporated by Pag Participa&#231;&#245;es.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:14.2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:14.2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">In April, 2025, PagSeguro Group constituted a new company as a subsidiary of PSHC called PSGP M&#233;xico Aggregator S. de R.L. de C.V (&#8220;PBMX M&#233;xico&#8221;) and is still pre-operational.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:14.2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:14.2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">The subsidiaries of PagSeguro Digital are PagSeg Participa&#231;&#245;es Ltda. (&#8220;PagSeguro Peru&#8221;) and PBMX M&#233;xico.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:57.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; font-size:2pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:57.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; font-size:1pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:14.2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">These unaudited condensed consolidated interim financial statements include BS Holding, PagSeg, Pag Participa&#231;&#245;es, PSHC and corresponding subsidiaries.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><div style="border-bottom:2.25pt solid #ffc000; padding-bottom:1pt"><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:7.1pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:-7.1pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; widows:0; orphans:0; font-size:13pt; -aw-outline-level:4"><font style="font-family:Arial; font-weight:bold">2. Presentation and preparation of the unaudited condensed consolidated interim financial statements and material accounting policies</font></p></div><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; widows:0; orphans:0"><font style="font-family:Arial; font-weight:bold; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:-7.1pt; line-height:normal; widows:0; orphans:0"><font style="font-family:Arial; font-weight:bold">2.1.</font><font style="width:3.45pt; text-indent:0pt; font-family:Arial; font-weight:bold; display:inline-block">&#160;</font><font style="font-family:Arial; font-weight:bold">Basis of preparation of the condensed consolidated interim financial information</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; widows:0; orphans:0"><font style="width:14.7pt; font-family:Arial; font-weight:bold; display:inline-block">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; font-size:12pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; font-size:11pt">These unaudited condensed consolidated interim financial statements have been prepared in accordance with International Accounting Standard 34, &#8220;Interim Financial Reporting&#8221; as issued by the International Accounting Standards Board (&#8220;IASB</font><font style="font-family:'Times New Roman'">&#174;</font><font style="font-family:Arial; font-size:11pt">&#8221;) and the International Financial Reporting Standards (&#8220;IFRS</font><font style="font-family:'Times New Roman'">&#174;</font><font style="font-family:Arial; font-size:11pt">&#8221;), disclose all (and only) the applicable significant information related to the financial statements, which is consistent with the information utilized by management in the performance of its duties. The consolidated interim financial statements are presented in thousands of Brazilian reais, unless otherwise indicated, which is the functional currency of PagSeguro Group.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; font-size:10pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">These unaudited condensed consolidated interim financial statements as of September 30, 2025 and for the three and nine-month period</font><font style="-aw-sdt-tag:'goog_rdk_67'"><font style="font-family:Arial">s</font></font><font style="font-family:Arial"> ended September 30, 2025 and 2024 (&#8220;Interim Financial Statements&#8221;) were authorized for issuance by the PagSeguro Digital&#8217;s Board of Directors on November 10, 2025.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; font-size:8pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; font-size:3pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">An entity shall include in its interim financial report an explanation of events and transactions that are significant to an understanding of the changes in financial position and performance of the entity since the end of the last annual reporting period. Information disclosed in relation to those events and transactions shall update the relevant information presented in the most recent annual financial report.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; font-size:8pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">These Interim Financial Statements do not include all the notes of the type normally included in an annual consolidated financial statement. Accordingly, this report is to be read in conjunction with the annual consolidated financial statements for the year ended December 31, 2024 (the &#8220;Annual Financial Statements&#8221;).</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; font:7pt 'Times New Roman'; display:inline-block; -aw-import:spaces">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font></font><font style="font-family:Arial">Amendment to IAS 21 &#8220;Lack of Exchangeability&#8221;: issued in August 2023, with the objective of clarifying entities to determine whether a currency is exchangeable into another currency, and which spot exchange rate to use when it is not. The amendments to IAS 21 are effective as of January 1, 2025. margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:8pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p></td></tr></table><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:6pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; font-size:7pt"><font style="font-family:Arial">(i) Card issuers: receivables derived from transactions where PagSeguro Brazil acts as the financial intermediary in operations with the issuing banks, related to the intermediation agreements between PagSeguro Brazil and Visa, Mastercard, Hipercard, Amex or Elo. However, PagSeguro Brazil&#8217;s contractual accounts receivable is with the financial institutions, which are the legal obligors on the accounts receivable payment. Additionally, amounts due within 27 days of the original transaction, including those that fall due with the first installment of installment receivables, are guaranteed by Visa, Mastercard, Hipercard, Amex or Elo, as applicable, if the legal obligors do not make the payment. Acquirers refers to card processing transactions to be received from the acquirers, which are a third parties acting as financial intermediaries between the issuing bank and PagSeguro Brazil. The Group has identified certain receivables from Card Issuers and Acquirers which are managed separately. The Group assessed that the appropriate business model of some Card Issuers and Acquirers originated after September of 2024 which is held by the Group as part of liquidity management is held to collect and sell and measured at FVOCI. Therefore, part of receivables, in the amount of R$3,819,897 (R$1,819,020 in December 31, 2024), changed from amortized cost to fair value through other comprehensive income. Unrealized loss in the accounts receivable mark-to-market, net of taxes, in the nine-month ended September 30, 2025, totaled R$95,376 (R$23,035 in the nine-month ended September 30, 2024).</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:6pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; font-size:7pt"><font style="font-family:Arial">(ii) Refers to other dispersed receivables from legal obligors.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:6pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; font-size:7pt"><font style="font-family:Arial">(iii) Payroll Loans, Credit Cards receivables and Other Loans are presented net of the ECL (&#8220;expected credit losses&#8221;) and are measured according to the IFRS 9, using: Exposure at Default (EAD) related to the exposed credit risk at default; Probability of Default (PD) related to the probability of the counterparty not meeting its contractual payment obligations; and Loss Given Default (LGD) related to the percentage of the exposure that is not expected to be recovered in the event of default. In addition to the methodology for calculating the allowance for impairment (EAD x PD x LGD). The Group takes into consideration the forward-looking information and assumptions such as the historical loss experienced at individual transactions level, credit quality and guarantees, economic factors and estimated future cash flows, which could impact the calculation model for provisioning expected credit losses.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:7.1pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:7.1pt; line-height:normal; font-size:10pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:spaces">&#160;</font><font style="font-family:Arial">The maturity analysis of accounts receivables are as follows:</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; -aw-import:spaces">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font></font><font style="font-family:Arial">Based on the PagSeguro credit risk classification model, which assesses the risk of insolvency and default of counterparties related to credit receivables, for the nine-month period ended September 30, 2025, the PagSeguro Group carried out a partial write-off of credit receivables, for cases in which the Group does not expect to receive these amounts. The credit card receivables were written-off in the amount of R$34,387 (R$138,505 on December 31, 2024), other loans were written-off in the amount R$5,890 (R$244,432 on December 31, 2024) and payroll loans were written-off in the amount R$14,216 (R$28,166 on December 31, 2024) against the related provision for ECL recognized in previous periods.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:10pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><div style="border-bottom:2.25pt solid #ffc000; padding-bottom:1pt"><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:18pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:-18pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; widows:0; orphans:0; font-size:13pt; -aw-outline-level:4"><font style="font-family:Arial; font-weight:bold">9. Recoverable taxes</font></p></div><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:8pt"><font style="font-family:'Times New Roman'; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><table cellspacing="0" cellpadding="0" style="width:96%; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:10pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><div style="border-bottom:2.25pt solid #ffc000; padding-bottom:1pt"><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:-21.3pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; widows:0; orphans:0; font-size:13pt; -aw-outline-level:4"><font style="font-family:Arial; font-weight:bold">14. Obligations to FIDC quota holders</font></p></div><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:10pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">In October 2022, 100,000 new senior quotas of the FIDC were issued with a nominal value of R$1,000 each, totaling R$100 million with third party investors. In September 2025, these quotas were redeemed in the amount of R$149,392 including interest.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">In November 2024, 1,000,000 new senior quotas of the FIDC were issued with a nominal value of R$1,000 each, totaling R$1 billion</font><font style="font-family:Arial">&#160;</font><font style="font-family:Arial">with an interest rate of 100% of the CDI plus a fixed rate of 1%. In the same operation, the Group entered swaps to change the interest rate accrual to 108% of the CDI. This operation has a specific objective of protect the risk from interest rate volatility for the investors remuneration changing fixed rates for CDI rates.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">Obligations to FIDC quotas holders are being disclosed separately in the amount of R$1,128,717 on September 30, 2025 (R$1,151,384 on December 31, 2024) with an average cost of 108% of CDI (108% of CDI on December 31, 2024). During the three and nine-month ended September 30, 2025 the remuneration refer the FIDC quotas holders amounted to R$47,578 and R$126,724 (R$3,949 and R$11,295 in the three and nine-month ended September 30, 2024).</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; For these certificates of deposit, the Group contracts derivative financial instruments (Swaps) designated to hedge accounting with the specific objective of protecting deposits from fluctuations arising from inflation, changing IPCA and fixed rates for CDI rates. More details of financial instruments in note 28.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:28.35pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; font-size:8pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:28.35pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:-14.15pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; font-size:8pt; -aw-import:list-item; -aw-list-level-number:0; -aw-list-number-format:'(%0)'; -aw-list-number-styles:'lowerRoman'; -aw-list-number-values:'2'; -aw-list-padding-sml:5.27pt"><font style="-aw-import:ignore"><font style="font-family:Arial">(ii)</font><font style="width:5.27pt; font:7pt 'Times New Roman'; display:inline-block; -aw-import:spaces">&#160;&#160;&#160; </font></font><font style="font-family:Arial">During the nine-month period ended September 30, 2025, the average interest cost associated amounted to 107% of CDI (110% of CDI on December 31, 2024), On September 30, 2025, the PagSeguro Group issued R$1,000,000 in Public Financial Letter. The maturity date will be July 10, 2027. The notional amount and accrued interest will be paid at maturity. The main tax lawsuits are disclosed below:</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; font-size:8pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">On October 15, 2021, Pagseguro Internet was assessed by the Brazilian Internal Revenue Service (&#8220;IRS&#8221;) for not collecting tax on financial operation (&#8220;IOF&#8221;) on intercompany loans, IOF is applicable</font><font style="font-family:Arial">&#160;</font><font style="font-family:Arial">over credit transactions of any nature, including intercompany loans.</font><font style="font-family:Arial">&#160;</font><font style="font-family:Arial">The amount of this assessment was</font><font style="font-family:Arial">&#160;</font><font style="font-family:Arial">R$336,022 (R$315,403 on December 31, 2024).</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; font-size:8pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">The Group has presented its defense, clarifying that the transactions carried out among PagSeguro and its subsidiaries are not credit transactions. The Pagseguro Group has a centralized cash pool and, according to the law, this kind of intercompany transaction is not taxable by IOF.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">Additionally, the Group has one contingency related to labor taxes in the amount of R$254,594 (R$234,120 on December 31, 2024).</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; widows:0; orphans:0; border-bottom:2.25pt solid #ffc000; padding-bottom:1pt; font-size:13pt; -aw-outline-level:4"><font style="font-family:Arial; font-weight:bold">20. -aw-import:spaces">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font></font><font style="font-family:Arial">Refers to the benefit granted by the Technological Innovation Law (</font><font style="font-family:Arial; font-style:italic">Lei do Bem</font><font style="font-family:Arial">), which reduces the income tax charges, based on the amount invested by the PagSeguro Group on specific intangible assets, see note 12.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:14.2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:-14.2pt; line-height:normal; font-size:8pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:14.2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:-14.2pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; font-size:8pt; -aw-import:list-item; -aw-list-level-number:0; -aw-list-number-format:'(%0)'; -aw-list-number-styles:'lowerRoman'; -aw-list-number-values:'2'; -aw-list-padding-sml:5.32pt"><font style="-aw-import:ignore"><font style="font-family:Arial">(ii)</font><font style="width:5.32pt; font:7pt 'Times New Roman'; display:inline-block; -aw-import:spaces">&#160;&#160;&#160; </font></font><font style="font-family:Arial">Some entities and investment funds adopt different taxation regimes according to the applicable rules in their jurisdictions, which differs from the Brazilian tax rate of 34% applied for the purpose of this note.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:50.2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:7pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; widows:0; orphans:0"><font style="font-family:Arial">Notes to the unaudited condensed consolidated interim financial statements</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; widows:0; orphans:0"><font style="font-family:Arial">As of September 30, 2025 and for the three and nine-month periods ended September 30, 2025 and 2024</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:14.2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:-14.2pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">(All amounts in thousands of reais unless otherwise stated)</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:14.2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:-14.2pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; font-size:13pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; font-weight:bold; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:14.2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:-14.2pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; font-size:13pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; font-weight:bold">22. Equity</font><font style="font-family:Arial"> (continued)</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">b)</font><font style="width:4.92pt; font-family:Arial; display:inline-block">&#160;</font><font style="font-family:Arial; text-decoration:underline">Capital reserve</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; font-size:5pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">The capital reserve can only be used to increase capital, offset losses, redeem, reimburse or purchase shares or pay cumulative dividends on preferred shares. For the nine-month periods ended September 30, 2025, and 2024, the Group recognized the capital reserve movement related to the costs of the FIDM and FIDC in the amount of R$1,762 (R$475 on December 31, 2024) and all the LTIP/ LTIP goals shares were delivered with treasury shares.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; font-size:10pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">c)</font><font style="width:5.54pt; font-family:Arial; display:inline-block">&#160;</font><font style="font-family:Arial; text-decoration:underline">Share based long-term incentive plan (LTIP and LTIP goals)</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; font-size:10pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">Under the terms of the LTIP, upon completion of the IPO, the vested portion of each beneficiary&#8217;s LTIP rights was converted into Class A common shares of PagSeguro Digital at the IPO price (US$21.50) which is the assessed fair value at the grant date. As a result, the beneficiaries of the LTIP received a total of 1,823,727 new Class A common shares upon completion of the IPO. </font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">LTIP-Goals was established by PagSeguro Brazil on December 18, 2018, as approved by the Company&#8217;s board of directors, modified and ratified on August 7, 2019, February 21, 2020, January 19, 2021, August 16, 2021, and December 20, 2021. Beneficiaries under the LTIP-Goals are selected by the LTIP-Goals Committee, which consists of the Company&#8217;s Chairman of the board of directors and two officers of UOL. </font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">The unvested portions of each beneficiary&#8217;s LTIP and LTIP goals rights will be settled on each future annual vesting date in cash, Class A common shares or a combination of the two.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; font-size:10pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">This arrangement is classified as equity settled. For the nine-month period ended September 30, 2025, the Group recognized in equity, costs related to the LTIP and LTIP Goals in the total amount of R$62,821 (R$135,012 in the nine-month period ended September 30, 2024). On September 30, 2025, the amount of R$37,720 (R$50,810 on December 31, 2024) was accounted for LTIP and LTIP Goals social charges, including withholding income tax (Note 17).</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">The maximum number of common shares that can be delivered to beneficiaries under the LTIP and LTIP Goals may not exceed 3% and 1% per year, respectively, of the PagSeguro Group&#8217;s issued share capital at any time. For the nine-month ended September 30, 2025, total shares delivered were 3,067,643 from treasury shares (3,200,293 for the nine-month ended September 30, 2024) representing 0.93% of total shares (1% for the nine-month ended September 30, 2024). Additionally total shares granted were 2,909,815 representing 0.95% of total shares.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:-21.25pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">d)</font><font style="width:4.92pt; font-family:Arial; display:inline-block">&#160;</font><font style="font-family:Arial; text-decoration:underline">OCI and equity valuation adjustments</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">The Group recognizes in this account the accumulated effect of the foreign exchange variation resulting from the conversion of the financial statements of the foreign subsidiaries BCPS, Pagseguro Colombia, Pagseguro Chile, Pagseguro Peru, Pagseguro Mexico and PBMX M&#233;xico which amounted to a loss of R$211 in the nine-month period ended September 30, 2025 (gain of R$798 in the nine-month period ended September 30, 2024). This accumulated effect will be reverted to the result of the year as gain or loss only in case of disposal or write-off of the investment.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:10pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><div style="-aw-sdt-tag:'PG_4f7181dc462bbadc'"><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:10pt"><font style="font-family:'Times New Roman'">29</font></p><div align="center"><hr size="1" align="center" style="width: 100%; Unrealized accumulated loss on LFTs for the nine-month period ended September 30, 2025 totaled R$110 (gain of R$627 in the nine-month period ended September 30, 2024) and the unrealized loss in the accounts receivable mark-to-market, net of taxes, in the nine-month period ended in September 30, 2025 totaled R$95,376 (R$23,035 in the nine-month period ended September 30, 2024).</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:14.2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; widows:0; orphans:0"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:14.2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; widows:0; orphans:0"><font style="font-family:Arial">The derivative financial instruments mentioned in note 20 were classified at fair value through other comprehensive income. Unrealized fair value adjustment loss on SWAPs, net of taxes, in the nine-month period ended September 30, 2025, totaled a loss of R$1,051 (loss of R$853 in the nine-month period ended September 30, 2024).</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:14.2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:9pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:14.2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; font-size:12pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; font-size:11pt">As part of transactions completed in prior years, the</font><font style="font-family:'Times New Roman'"> </font><font style="font-family:Arial; font-size:11pt">PagSeguro Group also recognized in this account the difference between the book value and the amounts paid in the acquisitions of additional interests from the non-controlling shareholders of the subsidiary represented by the accumulated amount of R$22,372 (R$22,372 as of September 30, 2024).</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:14.2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:8pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:14.2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">e)</font><font style="width:12.02pt; font-family:Arial; display:inline-block">&#160;</font><font style="font-family:Arial; text-decoration:underline">Treasury shares</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:14.2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:8pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:14.2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">On August 2024, The Board of directors has authorized a share repurchase program, under which PagSeguro Digital Ltd. may repurchase up to US$200 million in outstanding Class A common shares. The former program (announced in 2018) was concluded after the repurchase of a total amount of US$250 million in Class A common shares. </font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:14.2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:14.2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">On May 29, 2025, The Board of directors has authorized its third share repurchase program, under which PagSeguro Digital Ltd. may repurchase up to US$ 200 million in outstanding Class A common shares. The former program (announced in August 2024) was concluded after the repurchase of a total amount of US$200 million in Class A common shares. The new repurchase program will go into effect immediately and does not have a fixed expiration date. The Company&#8217;s management is responsible for defining the timing and the number of shares to be acquired, within authorized limits.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:14.2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:14.2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">On May 13, 2025, the Company&#8217;s Board of Directors approved the cancellation of 23,930,715 common shares held in treasury, in the total amount of R$1,208,680. As a result of cancellation PagSeguro&#8217;s share capital will be comprised of 305,677,709.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:10pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; The dividends were paid on August 13, 2025, totaling R$195,164, being R$81,200 to UOL and R$113,964 to third-party shareholders. </font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:14.2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:14.2pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">On September 3, 2025 the Company&#8217;s Board of Directors approved the third payment of dividend of US$0.12 per common share of the Company. The dividends provisioned will be paid on November 3, 2025, to shareholders of record as of October 6, 2025 totaling R$185,714, being R$77,505 to UOL and R$108,209 to third-party shareholders. The provision is an estimate subject to small fluctuations caused by the exchange rate at the payment date, the final amount as disclosed in note 31.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; font-size:10pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:12pt"><font style="font-family:'Times New Roman'; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; widows:0; orphans:0; border-bottom:2.25pt solid #ffc000; padding-bottom:1pt; font-size:13pt; -aw-outline-level:4"><font style="font-family:Arial; font-weight:bold">23. Earnings per share</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:4pt"><font style="font-family:'Times New Roman'; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:42.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:-21pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; -aw-import:list-item; -aw-list-level-number:0; -aw-list-number-format:'%0)'; -aw-list-number-styles:'lowerLetter'; -aw-list-number-values:'1'; -aw-list-padding-sml:11.22pt"><font style="-aw-import:ignore"><font style="font-family:Arial">a)</font><font style="width:11.22pt; font:7pt 'Times New Roman'; display:inline-block; -aw-import:spaces">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font></font><font style="font-family:Arial; text-decoration:underline">Basic</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:42.55pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; font-size:7pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:42.55pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">Basic earnings per share is calculated by dividing net income attributable to equity holders of PagSeguro Digital by the weighted average number of common shares issued and outstanding for the three and nine-month periods ended September 30, 2025 and 2024: </font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; The shares in the LTIP and LTIP Goals are the only shares with potential dilutive effect. Additionally, has assets amortizations of acquired companies in the amount of R$5,408 and R$16,224 in the three and nine-month periods ended September 30, 2025 (R$5,408 and R$16,225 in the three and nine-month periods ended September 30, 2024).</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:49.65pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:3pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:49.65pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:-14.15pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; font-size:8pt; -aw-import:list-item; -aw-list-level-number:0; -aw-list-number-format:'(%0)'; -aw-list-number-styles:'lowerRoman'; -aw-list-number-values:'3'; -aw-list-padding-sml:3.49pt"><font style="-aw-import:ignore"><font style="font-family:Arial">(iii)</font><font style="width:3.49pt; font:7pt 'Times New Roman'; display:inline-block; -aw-import:spaces">&#160; </font></font><font style="font-family:Arial">PagSeguro Brazil has a tax benefit on PIS and COFINS that allows it to reduce the depreciation and amortization over some operational expenses when incurred. This tax benefit is recognized directly as a reduction of depreciation and amortization expenses.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:10pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; Therefore, the estimates presented may not necessarily indicate the amounts that could be obtained in the current market. The use of different hypotheses to calculate market value or fair value may have a material impact on the amounts obtained. The assets and liabilities presented in this note were selected based on their relevance.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:6pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">The PagSeguro Group believes that the financial instruments recognized in these consolidated interim financial statements at their carrying amount are substantially similar to their fair value. vertical-align:bottom"><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:13pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; font-weight:bold">PagSeguro Digital Ltd.</font></p></td><td style="width:50%; vertical-align:top"><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:right; line-height:normal; font-size:13pt"><img src="c596ca262bf13cd292a1.jpg" alt="Graphics" style="-aw-left-pos:0pt; -aw-rel-hpos:column; -aw-rel-vpos:paragraph; -aw-top-pos:0pt; -aw-wrap-type:inline;height:50;width:132;"></p></td></tr><tr><td style="width:50%; vertical-align:top"><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; font-size:13pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; font-weight:bold; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p></td><td style="width:50%; vertical-align:top"><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; font-size:13pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; font-weight:bold; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p></td></tr></table><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; widows:0; orphans:0"><font style="font-family:Arial">Notes to the unaudited condensed consolidated interim financial statements</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; widows:0; orphans:0"><font style="font-family:Arial">As of September 30, 2025 and for the three and nine-month periods ended September 30, 2025 and 2024</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">(All amounts in thousands of reais unless otherwise stated)</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:12pt"><font style="font-family:'Times New Roman'; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; widows:0; orphans:0; border-bottom:2.25pt solid #ffc000; padding-bottom:1pt; font-size:13pt; -aw-outline-level:4"><font style="font-family:Arial; font-weight:bold">27. Financial risk management</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; widows:0; orphans:0; font-size:8pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">The PagSeguro Group&#8217;s activities expose it to a variety of financial risks: market risk, fraud risk (total losses), credit risk and liquidity risk. The PagSeguro Group&#8217;s overall risk management program focuses on the unpredictability of financial markets and seeks to minimize potential adverse effects on the PagSeguro Group&#8217;s financial performance.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; text-decoration:underline">Market risk</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:8pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">Market risk is the risk that the fair value or future cash flows of a financial instrument will fluctuate because of changes in market prices. In the Pagseguro Group, market risk comprises interest rate risk and foreign currency risk and other price risk, such as equity price risk.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; text-decoration:underline">Interest rate risk</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:8pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">Interest rate risk is the risk that the fair value or future cash flows of a financial instrument will fluctuate because of changes in market interest rates. The Pagseguro Group&#8217;s exposure to the risk of changes in market interest rates arises primarily from financial investments and deposits both subject to variable interest rates, principally the CDI rate. The Pagseguro Group conducted a sensitivity analysis for the following twelve month of the interest rate risks to which the financial instruments are exposed as of September 30, 2025. For this analysis, the Pagseguro Group adopted a probable scenario maintaining the actual interest rates of 14.90% for the CDI and two simulations with a 100 bps to increase and decrease with a interest rates of 15.90% and 13.90% of the CDI, respectively. As a result, financial income (with respect to financial investments) and financial expense (with respect to certificate of deposit, corporate securities, banking accounts and interbank deposits) would be impacted as follows:</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><table cellspacing="0" cellpadding="0" style="width:97%; margin-left:14.2pt; border-collapse:collapse"><tr style="height:11.5pt; page-break-inside:avoid"><td style="width:34%; border-bottom:0.75pt solid #000000; padding-right:3.5pt; padding-left:3.5pt; vertical-align:middle; -aw-border-bottom:0.5pt single"><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:8pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; font-weight:bold">Transaction</font></p></td><td style="width:13.64%; border-bottom:0.75pt solid #000000; padding-right:3.5pt; padding-left:3.5pt; vertical-align:middle; -aw-border-bottom:0.5pt single"><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:8pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; font-weight:bold">Interest rate risk</font></p></td><td style="width:10%; border-bottom:0.75pt solid #000000; padding-right:3.5pt; padding-left:3.5pt; vertical-align:middle; -aw-border-bottom:0.5pt single"><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:8pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; font-weight:bold">Book Value</font></p></td><td style="width:14%; border-bottom:0.75pt solid #000000; padding-right:3.5pt; padding-left:3.5pt; vertical-align:middle; -aw-border-bottom:0.5pt single"><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:8pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; font-weight:bold">Probable scenario with maintaining of CDI (14.90%)</font></p></td><td style="width:14%; border-bottom:0.75pt solid #000000; font-weight:bold">27. Financial risk management</font><font style="font-family:Arial"> (continued)</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; text-decoration:underline">Foreign exchange risk</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">Foreign currency risk is the risk that the fair value or future cash flows of an exposure will fluctuate because of changes in foreign exchange rates. The Pagseguro Group&#8217;s exposure to the risk when future commercial transactions or recognized assets or liabilities are denominated in a currency that is not the entity&#8217;s functional currency. The Company&#8217;s risk is mainly related to POS purchases. Pagseguro Tecnologia, BCPS, </font><font style="font-family:ArialMT">PSGP Mexico</font><font style="font-family:Arial">, Pagseguro Colombia, Pagseguro Chile and Pagseguro Peru that have revenues in other currencies and cash and cash equivalents maintained in other countries foreign currency exposure generated in companies like PagSeguro Colombia, PagSeguro Chile, are being hedged through a non-derivative forward.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; text-decoration:underline">Equity price risk</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:8pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">The Pagseguro Group&#8217;s non-listed equity investments are susceptible to market price risk arising from uncertainties about future values of the investment. As of September 30, 2025, and December 31, 2024, the exposure to equity price from such investments was not material.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; text-decoration:underline">Fraud risk (chargeback)</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:8pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">The PagSeguro Group&#8217;s sales transactions are susceptible to potentially fraudulent or improper sales and it uses the following two processes to control the fraud risk: </font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:6pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; -aw-import:list-item; -aw-list-level-number:0; -aw-list-number-format:'(%0)'; -aw-list-number-styles:'lowerRoman'; -aw-list-number-values:'1'; -aw-list-padding-sml:4.93pt"><font style="-aw-import:ignore"><font style="font-family:Arial">(i)</font><font style="width:4.93pt; font:7pt 'Times New Roman'; display:inline-block; -aw-import:spaces">&#160;&#160; </font></font><font style="font-family:Arial">The first process consists of monitoring, on a real time basis, the transactions carried out with credit and debit cards and payment slips, through an anti-fraud system. This process approves or rejects suspicious transactions at the time of the authorization, based on statistical models that are revised on a periodic basis.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:8pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:6pt; text-indent:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; -aw-import:list-item; -aw-list-level-number:0; -aw-list-number-format:'(%0)'; -aw-list-number-styles:'lowerRoman'; -aw-list-number-values:'2'; -aw-list-padding-sml:2.49pt"><font style="-aw-import:ignore"><font style="font-family:Arial">(ii)</font><font style="width:2.49pt; font:7pt 'Times New Roman'; display:inline-block; -aw-import:spaces">&#160; </font></font><font style="font-family:Arial">The second process detects chargebacks and disputes not identified by the first process. This is a supplemental process and increases the PagSeguro Group&#8217;s ability to avoid new frauds. PagSeguro&#8217;s expenses with chargebacks are disclosed in note 25.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:2pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; text-decoration:underline">Credit risk</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">Credit risk is the risk that a counterparty will not meet its obligations under a financial instrument or customer contract, leading to a financial loss. The Pagseguro Group&#8217;s is exposed to credit risk from its operating activities (primarily accounts receivable) and from its financing activities, including deposits with banks and financial institutions, and other financial instruments such as loans and credit card receivables with the Company&#8217;s customers.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">Credit risk is managed on a group basis and for its accounts receivable is limited to the possibility of default by: (a) the card issuers, which have the obligation of transferring to the credit and debit card labels the fees charged for the transactions carried out by their card holders, (b) the acquirers, which are used by the PagSeguro Group&#8217;s to approve transactions with the issuers and (c) analyses for the customers background to provide access to credit portfolio.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:10pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><div style="-aw-sdt-tag:'PG_0fa956163eccb90d'"><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:10pt"><font style="font-family:'Times New Roman'">37</font></p><div align="center"><hr size="1" align="center" style="width: 100%; color: #000000; background-color: #000000; margin: 1rem 0;"/></div></div></div><div class="Section_39"><table cellspacing="0" cellpadding="0" style="width:100%; border-collapse:collapse"><tr style="height:40.95pt"><td style="width:50%; vertical-align:bottom"><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:13pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; font-weight:bold">PagSeguro Digital Ltd.</font></p></td><td style="width:50%; vertical-align:top"><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:right; line-height:normal; font-size:13pt"><img src="c596ca262bf13cd292a1.jpg" alt="Graphics" style="-aw-left-pos:0pt; -aw-rel-hpos:column; -aw-rel-vpos:paragraph; -aw-top-pos:0pt; -aw-wrap-type:inline;height:50;width:132;"></p></td></tr><tr><td style="width:50%; vertical-align:top"><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; font-size:13pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; font-weight:bold; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p></td><td style="width:50%; vertical-align:top"><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; font-size:13pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; font-weight:bold; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p></td></tr></table><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; widows:0; orphans:0"><font style="font-family:Arial">Notes to the unaudited condensed consolidated interim financial statements</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; widows:0; orphans:0"><font style="font-family:Arial">As of September 30, 2025 and for the three and nine-month periods ended September 30, 2025 and 2024</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">(All amounts in thousands of reais unless otherwise stated)</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:13pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; font-weight:bold; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:13pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; font-weight:bold">27. Financial risk management</font><font style="font-family:Arial"> (continued)</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">In order to mitigate this risk, PagSeguro Brazil has established a Credit Committee, whose responsibility is to assess the level of risk of each of the card issuers served by PagSeguro Group, classifying them into three groups: </font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:6pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; -aw-import:list-item; -aw-list-level-number:0; -aw-list-number-format:'(%0)'; -aw-list-number-styles:'lowerRoman'; -aw-list-number-values:'1'; -aw-list-padding-sml:4.93pt"><font style="-aw-import:ignore"><font style="font-family:Arial">(i)</font><font style="width:4.93pt; font:7pt 'Times New Roman'; display:inline-block; -aw-import:spaces">&#160;&#160; </font></font><font style="font-family:Arial">Card issuers with a low level of risk, with credit ratings assigned by FITCH, S&amp;P and Moody&#8217;s, which do not require additional monitoring; and</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:6pt; text-indent:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; -aw-import:list-item; -aw-list-level-number:0; -aw-list-number-format:'(%0)'; -aw-list-number-styles:'lowerRoman'; -aw-list-number-values:'2'; -aw-list-padding-sml:2.49pt"><font style="-aw-import:ignore"><font style="font-family:Arial">(ii)</font><font style="width:2.49pt; font:7pt 'Times New Roman'; display:inline-block; -aw-import:spaces">&#160; </font></font><font style="font-family:Arial">Card issuers with a medium level of risk, which are also monitored in accordance with the financial metrics and ratios; and</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:35.45pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:-14.15pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; -aw-import:list-item; -aw-list-level-number:0; -aw-list-number-format:'(%0)'; -aw-list-number-styles:'lowerRoman'; -aw-list-number-values:'3'; -aw-list-padding-sml:0.04pt"><font style="-aw-import:ignore"><font style="font-family:Arial">(iii)</font><font style="width:0.04pt; font:7pt 'Times New Roman'; display:inline-block"> </font></font><font style="font-family:Arial"> Card issuers with a high level of risk, which are assessed by the committee at monthly meetings.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; widows:0; orphans:0; -aw-outline-level:4"><font style="font-family:Arial; font-weight:bold; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">PagSeguro Group has a rating process for loans and credit, based on statistical application models (in the early stages of customer relationships) and behavior scoring (used for customers who already have a relationship history). A process for designing, calibrating, and implementing policies and guidelines for granting credit and calibrating collection rules.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">A process for monitoring the portfolio&#8217;s risk profile, with a prospective view, which generates early warning feedbacks to the credit granting policies and risk classification models in a timely manner.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:10pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; text-decoration:underline">Liquidity risk</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:10pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">The PagSeguro Group manages liquidity risk by maintaining reserves, bank and credit lines in order to obtain borrowings, when deemed appropriate. The PagSeguro Group continuously monitors actual and projected cash flows and matches the maturity profile of its financial assets and liabilities in order to ensure that the PagSeguro Group has enough funds to honor its obligations to third parties and meet its operational needs.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:10pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">The PagSeguro Group invests surplus cash in interest bearings financial investments, choosing instruments with appropriate maturity or enough liquidity to provide adequate margin as determined by the forecasts. On September 30, 2025, PagSeguro Group held cash and cash equivalents of R$1,427,303 (R$927,668 on December 31, 2024).</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">The table below shows the PagSeguro Group&#8217;s non-derivative financial liabilities divided into the relevant maturity group based on the remaining period from the balance sheet date and the contractual maturity date. The amounts disclosed in the table are the contractual undiscounted cash flows.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:10pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><div style="-aw-sdt-tag:'PG_b2d8cf6d7dcd70ae'"><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:10pt"><font style="font-family:'Times New Roman'">38</font></p><div align="center"><hr size="1" align="center" style="width: 100%; padding-right:3.5pt; padding-left:3.5pt; vertical-align:middle"><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:right; line-height:normal; font-size:8pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p></td><td style="width:13.48%; padding-right:3.5pt; padding-left:3.5pt; vertical-align:middle"><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:right; line-height:normal; font-size:8pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:spaces">&#160;</font><font style="font-family:Arial">5,435,056 </font></p></td><td style="width:1.4%; padding-right:3.5pt; padding-left:3.5pt; vertical-align:middle"><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:right; line-height:normal; font-size:8pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p></td><td style="width:13.68%; padding-right:3.5pt; padding-left:3.5pt; vertical-align:middle"><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:right; line-height:normal; font-size:8pt"><font style="font-family:Arial">806,348 </font></p></td><td style="width:1.4%; padding-right:3.5pt; padding-left:3.5pt; vertical-align:middle"><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:right; line-height:normal; font-size:8pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p></td><td style="width:13.68%; padding-right:3.5pt; padding-left:3.5pt; vertical-align:middle"><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:right; line-height:normal; font-size:8pt"><font style="font-family:Arial">2,603,457 </font></p></td><td style="width:1.4%; padding-right:3.5pt; padding-left:3.5pt; vertical-align:middle"><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:right; line-height:normal; font-size:8pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p></td><td style="width:13.68%; padding-right:3.5pt; padding-left:3.5pt; vertical-align:middle"><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:right; line-height:normal; font-size:8pt"><font style="font-family:Arial">12,943,828 </font></p></td></tr></table><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:10pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; text-decoration:underline">Social, environmental and climate risks</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:7pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">Social, environmental and climate risks are the possibility of losses due to exposure to events of social, environmental and/or climate origin related to the activities carried out by the PagSeguro Group. Management evaluated the social, environmental and climate factors in which its businesses are inserted and considers them to have a low impact on the creation of shared value in the short, medium, and long term.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">In the specific case of climate risks, they are divided into two categories: (i) physical risks, stemming from changes in weather patterns, such as increased rainfall, droughts, and extreme climate events, and (ii) transition risks, related to impacts associated with adaptation to a low-carbon economy, including new regulations, technological changes, and shifts in consumer preferences. For the purposes of climate risk analysis, the Group uses the Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures (TCFD) methodology and the methodologies within the Central Bank&#8217;s regulatory framework.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">Despite this, to mitigate social, environmental and climate risks, actions are carried out to analyze processes, risks and controls, follow up on new rules related to the topic and record occurrences in internal systems. In addition to identification, the stages of prioritization, risk response, mitigation, monitoring and reporting of assessed risks complement the management of this risk at the PagSeguro Group.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:12pt"><font style="font-family:'Times New Roman'; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><div style="-aw-sdt-tag:'PG_c263779a0284e2e6'"><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:10pt"><font style="font-family:'Times New Roman'">39</font></p><div align="center"><hr size="1" align="center" style="width: 100%; color: #000000; background-color: #000000; Derivative Financial Instruments designated to Hedge Accounting</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:9pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">The Pagseguro Group trades derivative financial instruments (SWAPs) to manage its overall exposures (foreign currency, inflation index and interest rate).</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:9pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:35.45pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-indent:-35.45pt; line-height:normal; -aw-import:list-item; -aw-list-level-number:0; -aw-list-number-format:'%0)'; -aw-list-number-styles:'lowerRoman'; -aw-list-number-values:'1'; -aw-list-padding-sml:15.19pt"><font style="-aw-import:ignore"><font style="font-family:Arial">i)</font><font style="width:15.19pt; font:7pt 'Times New Roman'; display:inline-block; -aw-import:spaces">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font></font><font style="font-family:Arial">Cash flow hedge</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:9pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">In December 2024 and January 2025, the PagSeguro Group entered in an EU&#8364;100 million and EU&#8364;150 million borrowings agreements, respectively, with a maturity of one-year from the execution date and the payments will be made with a single instalment as the due date. In both operations, the Company contracted into a swaps, with the specific objective to protect said borrowings from fluctuations arising from exchange variation, changing the risk to CDI. All the amounts are covered with the derivatives and the same due date is applied. </font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:12pt"><font style="font-family:'Times New Roman'; -aw-list-number-values:'2'; -aw-list-padding-sml:5.32pt"><font style="-aw-import:ignore"><font style="font-family:Arial">(ii)</font><font style="width:5.32pt; font:7pt 'Times New Roman'; display:inline-block; -aw-import:spaces">&#160;&#160;&#160; </font></font><font style="font-family:Arial">In the balance sheet the amounts presented in derivative financial instruments include others financial instruments not-designated to hedge accounting. </font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:8pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">The PagSeguro Group issued certificate of deposits with interest rates correlated to the IPCA (Brazilian inflation rates) and interest fixed rates. For these certificates of deposits, the Group entered into swaps with the specific objective of protecting said deposits from fluctuations arising from inflation and high interest rates, changing them for CDI rates. All the amounts, which include principal and interest, are covered and the same due dates are applied. Derivative Financial Instruments designated to Hedge Accounting </font><font style="font-family:Arial">(continued)</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; widows:0; orphans:0; -aw-outline-level:4"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; widows:0; orphans:0; -aw-outline-level:4"><font style="font-family:Arial">The structure of risk limits is extended to the risk factor level, where specific limits aim at improving the monitoring and understanding processes, as well as avoiding concentration of these risks, Additionally, as the main financial assets and financial liabilities of the Group are measured by CDI, the PagSeguro Group&#8217;s strategy is to change any other risk factors to CDI</font><font style="font-family:Arial; font-weight:bold">. </font><font style="font-family:Arial">The PagSeguro Group undertakes risk management through the economic relationship between hedge instruments and hedged item, in which it is expected that these instruments will move in opposite directions, in the same proportions, with the aim of neutralizing the risk factors. The Group performs the hedging account effectiveness as each reporting date test and for the three and nine-month period ended September 30, 2025 and the year ended December 31, 2024, these tests were effective.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:12pt"><font style="font-family:'Times New Roman'; font-weight:bold; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; widows:0; orphans:0; border-bottom:2.25pt solid #ffc000; padding-bottom:1pt; font-size:13pt; -aw-outline-level:4"><font style="font-family:Arial; font-weight:bold">29. padding-right:3.5pt; padding-left:3.5pt; vertical-align:middle"><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:right; line-height:normal; font-size:8pt"><font style="font-family:Arial">3,981</font></p></td></tr></table><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:12pt"><font style="font-family:'Times New Roman'; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; widows:0; orphans:0; border-bottom:2.25pt solid #ffc000; padding-bottom:1pt; font-size:13pt; -aw-outline-level:4"><font style="font-family:Arial; font-weight:bold">30. Fair value measurement</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:5pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:5pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal"><font style="font-family:Arial">Fair value is the price that would be received to sell an asset or paid to transfer a liability (exit price) in the principal or most advantageous market for the asset or liability, in an orderly transaction between market participants at the measurement date. A three-level hierarchy is used to measure fair value, as shown below:</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:6pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:6pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; -aw-import:list-item; -aw-list-level-number:0; -aw-list-number-format:'&#9679;'; -aw-list-number-styles:'bullet'; -aw-list-padding-sml:8.1pt"><font style="-aw-import:ignore"><font style="font-family:'Noto Sans Symbols'">&#9679;</font><font style="width:8.1pt; font:7pt 'Times New Roman'; display:inline-block; -aw-import:spaces">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font></font><font style="font-family:Arial">Level 1 - Quoted prices (unadjusted) in active markets for identical assets and liabilities.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:6pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; -aw-import:list-item; -aw-list-level-number:0; -aw-list-number-format:'&#9679;'; -aw-list-number-styles:'bullet'; -aw-list-padding-sml:8.1pt"><font style="-aw-import:ignore"><font style="font-family:'Noto Sans Symbols'">&#9679;</font><font style="width:8.1pt; font:7pt 'Times New Roman'; display:inline-block; -aw-import:spaces">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font></font><font style="font-family:Arial">Level 2 - Inputs other than quoted prices included within Level 1 that are observable for the asset or liability, either directly (that is, as prices) or indirectly (that is, derived from prices).</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; -aw-import:list-item; -aw-list-level-number:0; -aw-list-number-format:'&#9679;'; -aw-list-number-styles:'bullet'; -aw-list-padding-sml:8.1pt"><font style="-aw-import:ignore"><font style="font-family:'Noto Sans Symbols'">&#9679;</font><font style="width:8.1pt; font:7pt 'Times New Roman'; display:inline-block; -aw-import:spaces">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font></font><font style="font-family:Arial">Level 3 - Inputs for the assets and liabilities that are not based on observable market data (that is, unobservable inputs).</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:10pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-left:21.3pt; margin-bottom:0pt; text-align:justify; line-height:normal; font-size:12pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; font-size:11pt">The PagSeguro Group believes that the financial instruments recognized in these consolidated interim financial statements at their carrying amount are substantially similar to its fair value. Regarding financial assets, they are comprised by accounts receivable from credit/debit card issuers and acquirers originated from transactions through PagSeguro Group payment platform</font><font style="font-family:Arial"> </font><font style="font-family:Arial; font-size:11pt">comprised of transactions approved by large financial institutions in the normal course of business. The financial investments are represented by government bonds with quoted prices in an active market and recognized in the balance sheet based on its fair value.</font></p><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:10pt"><font style="font-family:Arial; -aw-import:ignore">&#160;</font></p><div style="-aw-sdt-tag:'PG_cc6a6a925a066098'"><p style="margin-top:0pt; margin-bottom:0pt; line-height:normal; font-size:10pt"><font style="font-family:'Times New Roman'">41</font></p><div align="center"><hr size="1" align="center" style="width: 100%; color: #000000; background-color: #000000; margin: 1rem 0;"/></div></div></div><div class="Section_43"><table cellspacing="0" cellpadding="0" style="width:100%;
